headline: content path (one-hot RAM read): SmoothGrad lands on the TRUE causal byte (precision@1=1); position output (ball_pixel): SmoothGrad VANISHES → near chance — plausible ≠ faithful. Smoothing denoises the visual map but does NOT change faithfulness (Δcorr≈0). Oracle-as-method positive control corr=1/p@k=1.

robustness_sigma_n_sweep.conclusion: smoothing improves visual cleanliness, NOT faithfulness — quantified.
robustness_sigma_n_sweep.measured: content-path corr is INVARIANT across the whole grid (max |Δcorr| ≈ 0 to 4 d.p.; e.g. qbert 0.9994 and pong 0.4072 at every σ/N).
robustness_sigma_n_sweep.why: soft_ram_peek is a one-hot read ⇒ ∂y/∂u is a CONSTANT (1 at the self-byte, 0 elsewhere) independent of the input, so averaging |∂y/∂u| over noisy copies returns the same constant. SmoothGrad therefore cannot improve (or harm) FAITHFULNESS on the VCS content path — it only denoises the visual map. Re-run with `--sigma-sweep` to reproduce.
